name: ANUBHAV SINHA
summary: Full-Stack Software Engineer with 3+ years of industry experience across Oracle and Microsoft, specializing in high-throughput distributed systems and full-stack product delivery. Backend-leaning across the stack — equally comfortable designing scalable low-latency microservices and shipping responsive, performant frontend. Proven ability to own cross-cutting concerns: in-memory caching, API design, Kubernetes deployments, and UI performance.

roles.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Designed and delivered a horizontally scalable event streaming pipeline (Kafka) to decouple a high-volume legacy processing path, using feature-flag-driven cutover (Helm toggles) to enable zero-downtime migration and reduce upstream database load by 35%.

Integrated a protocol translation layer into a legacy monolith to decouple core business logic from evolving network interfaces, enabling the system to absorb future protocol changes without service-layer modifications, sustaining 10k RPS at under 5ms latency overhead.

Architected configurable alerting workflows with deduplication logic, reducing redundant Kafka message volume by 27%; redesigned service mounting and initialization mechanisms to accelerate deployment pipelines by 25% while maintaining full backward compatibility.

Led frontend modernization of a legacy enterprise dashboard, migrating to Next.js and a modular component architecture — improving perceived load time by 14% and increasing user task-completion rate by 20%.

Built RESTful APIs and reusable UI components to automate high-variety file ingestion workflows, cutting manual processing time by 95%; leveraged Oracle Coherence distributed caching to serve real-time records at low latency under high concurrency.

Owned production reliability across 100+ incidents (39 P0/P1), reducing critical backlog by 31%; proactively documented root-cause patterns and authored runbooks to prevent recurrence of top failure classes.

roles.title: Software Engineer Intern
bullets: Spearheaded a Java 8-to-21 migration, resolving transitive dependency conflicts to eliminate 100% of critical CVEs; built an internal CLI utility to abstract complex build commands, cutting build time by 20% and reducing onboarding friction for new contributors.
Authored a comprehensive test suite for a newly decoupled microservice (Groovy/Spock), raising code coverage from 56% to 92% and establishing a testing baseline for the service.
Executed and validated hybrid deployments (on-prem and Kubernetes via Helm) for a high-throughput service, running load tests to confirm sub-50ms latency at peak concurrency.
roles.techStack: Java, JavaScript, Spring, Helidon, React.js, Next.js, Kafka, Groovy, Spock, Cucumber, Kubernetes, Helm

roles.title: Software Engineer Intern
bullets: Resolved a performance regression in Microsoft Loop's real-time collaboration layer, reducing round-trip latency by 25% — directly improving editing responsiveness for users on the collaborative canvas.
Extended the people picker component with configurable schema support, enabling flexible data integration for custom user contexts and reducing associated processing overhead by 35%.

Re-implemented 100% text-editing functionality following a breaking dependency removal, systematically identifying and resolving regressions to restore feature parity — eliminating a critical blocker for the Loop editing experience.

skills.items: Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C/C++, Python, Groovy, HTML/CSS
Spring Boot, Spring, Helidon, Node.js, Express, REST APIs
Apache Kafka, Oracle Coherence, Microservices, Event-Driven Architecture
Oracle NoSQL, MySQL, Postgres, MongoDB, Firebase
Kubernetes, Docker, Helm, CI/CD, Generative AI, Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
Spock, Cucumber, Unit Testing, Integration Testing
System Design Principles, API Design, Performance Optimization, Problem Solving, Data Structures, Algorithms

projects.title: Resonance
bullets: Created the full-stack MVP in 10 weeks: set up JWT auth, protected routes (React Context API), and backend middleware for request handling, auth flow, validation, and error handling; primary owner of front-end and middleware.

Built RESTful APIs and data model for internship listings (15 API endpoints, 20+ React components); applied parameterized queries, server-side pagination, and MongoDB indexing to support efficient filtering and large result sets.

achievements: Rated 1800+ and solved 700+ LeetCode problems. Global Rank 135 in CodeChef September Long Challenge (6k+ participants).
Drove 60+ member team as Vice-chairman of IEEE (student body), managing events, budgets, workshops, and external collaborations.
Winner, Student Branch Associates Program (AISYWLC'20) — IEEE Delhi Section and Winner, IEEE LEAD'20 web development contest.
